{{Quote|What's with the Moff-sized egos?|[[Gunn Yage]]|<ref name="Legacy (2006) 14">[[Legacy (2006) 14|''Legacy'' (2006) 14]]</ref>}}
'''1st Imperial Center Core Defense Squadron''', better known as '''Skull Squadron''', was an elite [[Predator-class fighter|''Predator''-class fighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. The squadron was initially loyal to [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Empire]] before defecting to [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]'s [[Empire-in-exile]] during the [[Attack on the Hidden Temple]].

==History==
At some point prior to the removal of [[Roan Fel]] from the [[Fel Empire|Imperial]] throne, Skull Squadron was commanded by [[Rulf Yage]], who eventually became [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] and the head of the Imperial Navy.

[[File:ImperialPilots.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] of Skull [[Squadron/Legends|Squadron]].]]
During the last days of the [[Sith–Imperial War]] Skull Squadron was assigned to protecting the convoy that brought [[Roan Fel]] and [[Roan Fel's double|his imposter]] to claim the recently captured [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="LECG">''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

During the [[Second Imperial Civil War]], Skull Squadron remained loyal to the usurper [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Darth Krayt]], and was permanently stationed at [[Skull Squadron Hangar]] on Coruscant, under the direct command of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Rulf Yage]]. It was at this time led by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Gunn Yage]], daughter of Rulf Yage and Moff [[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]], while other [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] in the unit included [[Jae Akura]], [[Brodie Coburn]], and [[Tev Rimon]].

Skull Squadron pursued the [[Mynock (Helot-class)|''Mynock'']], but lost it in the lower levels of Coruscant. They later [[Skirmish at the Temple of the Sith|confronted]] it at the [[Temple of the Sith]] but were forced to pull back under orders of [[High Moff]] [[Morlish Veed]].

Some time afterward, the Skulls were assigned to support the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] pilots of the [[First Sith Imperial Strikeforce]] sent by [[Regent/Legends|Regent]] [[Morlish Veed]] to capture [[Roan Fel]] as part of [[Operation: Thunderstroke]]. The Skulls resented being relegated to a role subservient to the Sith and being forced by [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lady]] [[Darth Rauder]] to pilot prototypes of [[Sith-Imperial starfighter|Sith-designed starfighters]] instead of their trusted Predators. The operation was a success with the capture of [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Marasiah Fel]].

The Squadron then took part in the [[Devastation of Da Soocha]].

The Skulls were then assigned to take part in Darth Krayt's [[Attack on the Hidden Temple|attack]] on the Jedi [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Hidden Temple]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taivas]]. During this battle however they become disgusted with the tactics of Darth Krayt when Krayt's [[Darth Krayt's Sith trooper|Sith Troopers]] caused the death of many Sith-Imperials as well as the killing of children, so they followed Captain Yage in defecting after she killed Darth Rauder. The Skulls retreated to [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]] with other defecting Sith-Imperials and their new allies.<ref name="War4">[[Legacy — War 4|''Legacy — War'' 4]]</ref>

The Skulls later participated in the [[Battle of Coruscant (Second Imperial Civil War)|Battle of Coruscant]], flying alongside [[Twin Suns Squadron|Twin Suns]] and [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]].<ref name="War5">[[Legacy — War 5|''Legacy — War'' 5]]</ref> While Twin Suns stayed behind to fight Annihilators, Rogue and Skull Squadrons had to destroy planetary targets on Coruscant. They were successful but at the cost of a Rogue, as the battle continued, Annihilators pounded the Squadrons taking more fighters. But with Skywalker killing Darth Krayt, all the Sith Troopers went mad and took suicide runs. This brought the end to the battle as the other Sith fled.
